title: Synthetic survival-data generator + >= 1 license-verified open teaching dataset (manifest entries)
context: Every worked example in a stats-for-clinicians explainer requires an open or synthetic dataset that passes the licensing & data gate (datagate-003). Synthetic data is unambiguously usable (no license concerns, no re-identification risk), so a committed synthetic survival-data generator is the safest foundation for the pilot explainers. Additionally, one or more license-verified open teaching survival datasets (such as the classic datasets distributed with open-source statistics packages) may be included for richer or more familiar examples, provided each dataset's license explicitly permits our teaching use and redistribution. This task delivers the data infrastructure that pilot explainers hr-004 and km-006 depend on.
objective: Build and commit a synthetic survival-data generator script that produces realistic survival datasets for teaching purposes, and identify and manifest-record >= 1 license-verified open teaching survival dataset that passes the data gate, so that pilot explainers have confirmed, licensed example data to build worked examples on.
acceptanceCriteria: A committed synthetic survival-data generator script produces realistic survival datasets (e.g. with configurable sample size, event rate, censoring fraction, group hazard ratio); outputs are deterministic given a fixed seed and are emitted in a standard format (e.g. CSV with columns: id, time, event, group).

The synthetic dataset generated by the script is recorded in the data manifest with: source ('synthetic — generated by <scriptRef>'), version (commit hash or date), license (CC0 or CC-BY-4.0), de-identification status ('synthetic by construction'), and redistribution permission ('yes — synthetic').

At least one license-verified open teaching survival dataset is evaluated, and if it passes the gate (license recorded, permits teaching use and redistribution), it is added to the manifest with: source (name + URL), version/snapshot (date or release), license (id + evidence URL), de-identification status, and redistribution permission.

Every dataset in the manifest has passed the PASS criterion from datagate-003 (license recorded + permits teaching use); any evaluated dataset that does not pass is documented as EXCLUDED with the reason.
The generator script is MIT-licensed; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; commit DCO signed-off.
The manifest entries are linked from tasks hr-004 and km-006's workedExample.datasetRef fields (or those tasks reference the manifest).
resources: tasks/stats-for-clinicians-datagate-003.json — licensing & data gate policy and manifest schema
PLAN.md — Data, licensing & compliance; in-bounds data classes
Classic open survival datasets distributed with open-source statistics packages (license to be verified per dataset)
Open-access aggregate survival/incidence summaries from SEER/GLOBOCAN-style public data sources
output: A committed synthetic survival-data generator script (MIT) and a data manifest with >= 1 synthetic dataset entry and >= 1 license-verified open teaching dataset entry (or a documented EXCLUDE reason if none pass). Both are ready for use by hr-004, km-006, and subsequent explainer tasks.
